subject: Cul-de-sac on Granville Ave still not plowed — 3 days now
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live at the end of the cul-de-sac on Granville near 25th Ave and we have not seen a plow in three days. My neighbor had to call an ambulance yesterday and the paramedics nearly got stuck getting out. I am not exaggerating. The street is completely packed with snow and ice. I pay my taxes and I expect this to be addressed before someone gets seriously hurt. Please send a crew out here as soon as possible.

subject: Cul-de-sac on Granville Ave still not plowed — 3 days now
body_clean: Ms. Wojcik, we had that cul-de-sac on the second-pass list after arterials were cleared. I'm dispatching a truck to Granville this afternoon — should be out there by 2 p.m. Cul-de-sacs take a dedicated run; they can't be hit on the main route. We'll get a full clear and a salt application done today.

subject: Plow clipped my fence at 2208 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Good morning Mayor, I want to report that a village plow hit the split-rail fence along the right side of my property at 2208 Harvard Ave during the storm early Tuesday morning. Three posts are snapped and about twenty feet of rail is on the ground. I have photos with timestamps if needed. I'm not trying to cause trouble — I just need to know the process for getting this repaired or reimbursed. Thank you.

subject: Plow clipped my fence at 2208 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mr. Tibbs, thank you for reaching out and we're sorry to hear about your fence. Please send your photos to Public Works at pw@bellwoodil.gov and reference the date and your address. The department will open a damage report and a supervisor will contact you within five business days to assess the claim. Mayor Okonkwo asked me to make sure this is handled promptly.

subject: Plow clipped my fence at 2208 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Thank you, Tina. I just emailed the photos over to Public Works with all the details. Appreciate the quick response — I'll wait to hear from the supervisor. Happy to let someone come by in person if that helps with the assessment.

subject: Ice on sidewalk near Metra station — someone is going to fall
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the sidewalk on the north side of the Metra UP-W station entrance has been solid ice since the freeze last week. I slipped there Monday on my way to the 7:18 train and barely caught myself on the railing. I'm 71 years old and I do not have the reflexes I used to. Other commuters have been tiptoeing around it too. Is there any salt or sand that can be applied to that stretch? This feels like a real liability.

subject: Ice on sidewalk near Metra station — someone is going to fall
body_clean: Ms. Ferreira, that Metra station sidewalk is on the village maintenance schedule, but after the freeze we should have hit it again. I'm sending a crew with cold-mix salt this morning to treat the ice and apply sand for traction. We'll also check the drain nearby — if it's clogged that's why we're getting sheet ice there. Thank you for flagging this; please stay safe.

subject: Side streets on the 2300 block of Morris Ave completely ignored
body_clean: Hello Mayor, I've been patient but it's been four days since the snowstorm and Morris Ave between 23rd and 24th is still untouched. Washington and St. Charles look fine — I get that those come first — but the side streets are a mess. Kids are walking in the street to get to the bus stop because the sidewalks and road edges are packed with hard snow. At minimum can we get a plow down here today? I've lived here twelve years and it's never been this long.

subject: Request for a salt box on St. Paul Ave near Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, the block of St. Paul Ave near Bellwood Ave has no salt box and after every storm we're all scrambling. A few neighbors have been chipping in for rock salt out of pocket, which isn't right. A couple of other blocks nearby seem to have the orange village boxes — is there any way to get one installed on our block? We have a lot of elderly residents who really struggle when the streets and sidewalks ice over. Would appreciate any help you can offer.
address_hint: St. Paul Ave at Bellwood Ave

subject: Plow keeps filling in my driveway apron — 1742 Frederick Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I cleared my driveway at 1742 Frederick Ave twice on Saturday, and both times the plow came through and dumped a wall of snow right back across the bottom of it. I understand the road has to get plowed, but the windrow that gets left is three feet tall and almost solid ice. I'm 66 with a bad back and this is genuinely dangerous for me. Is there anything the village can do — maybe a secondary pass to clear the aprons on residential streets?

subject: Intersection of Bohland and 19th Ave icing over repeatedly
body_clean: Hello, I wanted to write in about the intersection at Bohland Ave and 19th Ave. There's a low spot there that collects meltwater and then refreezes overnight. I've seen at least two cars slide through the stop sign in the past week alone. Monday morning it was especially bad. I don't know if this is a drainage issue or just needs more salt treatments, but something needs to change at that corner before there's a real accident. Thank you.

subject: Eastern Ave not plowed by school start time
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. I'm a parent and I live on Eastern Ave near 32nd. The street wasn't plowed until almost 9:30 this morning, well after my kids had to leave for school. They were walking in snow up past their ankles in the roadway because the sidewalk was even worse. I know storms are unpredictable but other streets nearby seemed to get plowed much earlier. Is there any priority given to routes near schools? If not, there should be.

subject: Eastern Ave not plowed by school start time
body_clean: Ms. Palumbo, thank you for writing and I completely understand your frustration — the safety of children walking to school is a top concern for me personally. I've asked Director DiMeo to review our snow route sequencing to see whether we can factor proximity to school walking routes into the priority order. I'll follow up once I have a concrete answer. I appreciate you bringing this to my attention.

subject: Salt box on Englewood Ave at 50th is always empty
body_clean: Hey Mayor, the salt box at the corner of Englewood and 50th has been empty since before Christmas. Every time I check it, nothing. My elderly mother lives on that block and I worry about her slipping. Other neighbors have noticed too. Can someone please make sure that box gets restocked regularly? I'm not asking for anything crazy, just some salt in the box so people can use it. Thanks.

subject: Metra station sidewalk — recurring ice problem
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I wrote in about this same spot two winters ago and I'm sorry to be back with the same complaint. The sidewalk along the east side of the Metra UP-W station is a sheet of ice again after last week's freeze. The village salted it once but the runoff from the station awning drips overnight and it refreezes by morning. Could we look at a more permanent fix — some sand or grit material that stays put, or maybe addressing that drain? Thank you for your time.

subject: Cul-de-sac on Hirsch Ave — are we on the plow route?
body_clean: Hi Mayor, I've been a resident of the Hirsch Ave cul-de-sac for about eight years and I've never been sure if we're actually on the village plow route or if we just get lucky sometimes. After this last storm the street was passable only because one of my neighbors has a snowblower and cleared it himself. That shouldn't be the case. Can you confirm whether this cul-de-sac is on a scheduled route, and if not, can we get added?

subject: Cul-de-sac on Hirsch Ave — are we on the plow route?
body_clean: Mr. Bassett, I pulled up our route map and Hirsch Ave cul-de-sac is on Route 7 — it's supposed to be serviced within 12 hours of a declared snow event. Something broke down in the execution last storm and I want to find out why. I'll look into it this week. In the meantime you are absolutely on the list and the crew supervisor for Route 7 knows to confirm completion before signing off.

subject: Still no plow on Madison St near 50th — two days after storm
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor. Writing from 5002 Madison St. We had a solid five inches Tuesday night and it is now Thursday afternoon — no plow has come down our block. I drive a low-clearance sedan and I cannot get out without bottoming out. I've been working from home to avoid it but I have a medical appointment tomorrow that I cannot miss. I've called the non-emergency line twice with no callback. Really hoping your office can help get a crew out here.

subject: Plow buried fire hydrant on Marshall Ave at 19th
body_clean: Mayor, I want to flag something that seems like a safety issue. After the plow came through the 1900 block of Marshall Ave last Thursday, there's a snow pile about four feet tall sitting right in front of the fire hydrant on the corner near 19th. I don't know if that hydrant can even be reached in an emergency right now. I've been waiting a few days thinking someone would clear it, but it's still there. Who do I contact, or can your office make sure this gets dug out?

subject: Ice on ADA ramp at Washington and 25th — wheelchair access blocked
body_clean: Good morning Mayor. The ADA curb ramp at the corner of Washington Blvd and 25th Ave has had a thick layer of ice on it all week. My wife uses a wheelchair and we had to go two blocks out of our way Monday because it was completely impassable. I understand plowing is a big job, but accessible infrastructure has to be part of the plan. Is there a process for prioritizing ADA ramps for salt treatment after storms?

subject: Ice on ADA ramp at Washington and 25th — wheelchair access blocked
body_clean: Mr. Savoie, thank you for bringing this to my attention. You're absolutely right that accessible ramps must be treated as part of our winter maintenance — that corner gets a lot of pedestrian traffic and your wife and others who depend on those ramps cannot be an afterthought. I've asked Director DiMeo to salt Washington and 25th today and to develop a checklist of high-priority ADA ramp locations that get treated along with intersections after each snow event. I'll follow up with you once that protocol is in place.

subject: Thank you for quick response on the cul-de-sac off Avon Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to send a quick thank-you. I emailed last week about our cul-de-sac off Avon Ave near Hirsch and a crew was out there within 24 hours. That kind of responsiveness really does make a difference — especially for the couple of older folks on our block who had been essentially snowed in. Please pass along my appreciation to whoever came out and made it happen. It's nice to see the village working well.
